overview: This four-color control deck leverages Jeskai and Black mana to stabilize against aggressive strategies and grind out value in longer matchups. By utilizing Stock Up for early resource acceleration and Jeskai Revelation for combat tricks and card draw, the deck maintains parity while searching for a finish. Inevitable Defeat serves as the primary board reset and card advantage engine, allowing you to outlast opponents in the Standard format.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and resource generation
Closing out games with Jeskai Revelation combat tricks and value loops
Stabilizing with Inevitable Defeat and grinding out incremental advantages

keep_criteria: Hands with early draw or interaction
Hands containing Stock Up or Jeskai Revelation
Hands with balanced land counts and mana curve
Keep hands with 4-5 lands, at least one turn-1 or turn-2 interaction (Inevitable Defeat or cheap removal), and a draw spell (Stock Up or Jeskai Revelation) if you have 3+ lands.
Prioritize keeping hands that can play a land and cast a cantrip or answer on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early answers or card draw
Hands with too many or too few lands
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands and no early interaction, or 2 lands with no spells.

early_game: Play lands and draw spells to stabilize
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's game plan
Prioritize finding Stock Up to accelerate resources
Turn 1: Play a land, hold priority for interaction or cast a cheap cantrip if you have 2+ lands.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast your first interaction or draw spell.
Sequence lands to ensure WU/B/R access by turn 3.
Use Stock Up early to filter into answers or threats, but avoid overcommitting mana if the board is unstable.
mid_game: Deploy Jeskai Revelation to generate value and combat tricks
Use Inevitable Defeat to clear boards and gain card advantage
Maintain hand size and resource parity
only spend removal on threats that can swing the game or enable opponent's win condition.
Use Stock Up to cycle dead cards into answers or threats.
Actively monitor and empty the opponent's graveyard with Jeskai Revelation to prevent recursion or combo enablers.
Commit threats only when you have answered their primary interaction or when you can safely trade removal for their key piece.
Hold back a removal spell for turn-3-4 finishers.
late_game: Grind out value with Jeskai Revelation's draw and pump
Use Inevitable Defeat as a recurring finisher or board clear
Close the game with efficient threats and combat math
Resilient creatures and Soldier tokens generated by Inevitable Defeat, combined with Jeskai Revelation for massive card advantage and graveyard exile.
Incremental pressure from tokens and efficient removal wins the race once the board is stabilized.
Go all-in when you have established board control, opponent has no sweepers or counters left, and you can safely cast Jeskai Revelation or a finisher without fear of being countered or removed.
Commit to trading removal efficiently to clear the path for your threats.

key_cards.name: Inevitable Defeat
key_cards.notes: Use to wipe aggressive boards or remove key threats while drawing a card to maintain parity

key_cards.name: Stock Up
key_cards.notes: Cast early to fetch lands and draw cards, enabling faster access to high-cost control spells

key_cards.name: Jeskai Revelation
key_cards.notes: Play to draw cards and pump creatures, using it to close games or outswing opponents in combat

sequencing_tips: Hold Inevitable Defeat until the opponent commits multiple creatures or a key threat
Cast Stock Up as early as possible to smooth out mana and draw into answers
Use Jeskai Revelation's combat trick after the opponent commits to blockers or attacks
Common mistakes include overusing Stock Up too early, ignoring graveyard recursion, and holding too much interaction until it's too late.
Unique interaction: Inevitable Defeat on your own creature creates a 1/1 Soldier token, which can be used as a blocker, a sacrifice target, or a win condition.
Jeskai Revelation specifically targets the opponent's graveyard, so use it to exile their recursion pieces and combo enablers.
Always track opponent's graveyard to maximize Jeskai Revelation's value and avoid dying to unexpected recursion.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and use Inevitable Defeat to reset the board
Race with Jeskai Revelation value and use Stock Up to outdraw them
Play for attrition, use Jeskai Revelation to generate card advantage, and time Inevitable Defeat carefully

Use multiple cheap spells and finishers to force counterspells, then capitalize on Jeskai Revelation to exile countered spells from the graveyard. Bypass counters with discard, uncounterable threats, or by casting spells in response to their counters.

Apply early pressure with interaction to stop mana dorks and land drops. Use Jeskai Revelation to exile ramp spells from the graveyard if they recur. Prioritize board wipes and hard removal to stop their big threats.

Win the card advantage war by prioritizing Jeskai Revelation to exile opponent's draw spells and recursion from the graveyard. Trade removal efficiently, favoring Inevitable Defeat on your own creatures to generate tokens and swing the board. Control the graveyard to deny their Jeskai Revelation value.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard recursion (Selesnya Landfall), stack interaction (Izzet Spells/Prowess), and early damage sources.
Key tells: Opponent holding up mana on turn 2-3 usually indicates a counter, removal, or finisher.
Track how many removal spells they've seen to gauge their graveyard safety and board control capacity.
